script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. June 20th, 2026.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。2026年6月20日です。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Morning, Alex. You look a bit... not well rested.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。少し…寝不足みたいね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, the baby was up at 3. And then 5. I'm on my second coffee already.
script.ja: ええ、赤ちゃんが3時に起きて。それからまた5時に。もう2杯目のコーヒーです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's normal. Mine did the same. It ends around two.
script.ja: それは普通よ。うちの子もそうだった。だいたい2歳くらいで落ち着くわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Two years old, you mean? Not 2 AM? Oh, that sounds like a long time from now.
script.ja: 2歳ってことですか？午前2時じゃなくて？ああ、それはまだ先が長いな。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yep. That's The Daily Tanu-chan Show for you. Reality.
script.ja: そうよ。それが『今日のタヌちゃんショー』。現実ね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Well, at least it's not boiling hot yet. Moderate rain today, they said.
script.ja: まあ、少なくともまだ茹だるような暑さじゃないわね。今日は小雨らしいわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Rain all day? Ah, 100% chance, right? I saw that. And pretty cool, too. Like 22 degrees high? Maybe 20 low.
script.ja: 一日中雨ですか？ああ、降水確率100%でしたっけ？見ました。それに結構涼しいですね。最高気温22度くらい？最低20度かな。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes. It feels like a proper rainy season day. Good for tea.
script.ja: ええ。まさに梅雨らしい一日ね。紅茶にはちょうどいいわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Good for coffee too. Especially after 3 AM.
script.ja: コーヒーにもいいですよ。特に午前3時以降は。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I read something kind of wild this morning. On this day last year, June 20, 2025, there was an EF5 tornado.
script.ja: 今朝、ちょっとすごいことを読んだんですよ。去年の今日、2025年6月20日に、EF5の竜巻があったって。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Oh, really? Where was that?
script.ja: ああ、そうなの？どこで？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: In North Dakota, I think. Enderlin. And it said it was the first EF5 in twelve years. What does EF5 even mean?
script.ja: ノースダコタだったかな。エンダーリン。そして、12年ぶりのEF5だって。EF5って一体どういう意味なんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: EF5 is the strongest kind. It stands for Enhanced Fujita. Basically, it means really, really powerful. Like, it can destroy concrete buildings.
script.ja: EF5は一番強いタイプよ。エンハンスド・フジタの略。要するに、本当にものすごくパワフルってこと。コンクリートの建物も破壊できるくらい。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow. That's... intense. I didn't know that.
script.ja: うわあ。それは…強烈ですね。知りませんでした。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's not something you want to experience. Very rare.
script.ja: 経験したいものじゃないわね。とても珍しいことよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I'm glad we don't get those here. Just typhoons, which are bad enough. Sometimes I think I'm starting to hallucinate from lack of sleep.
script.ja: ええ、日本ではああいうのがなくて良かった。台風だけでも十分大変なのに。時々、寝不足で幻覚を見始めてるんじゃないかと思いますよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Hallucinate? You mean see things that aren't there?
script.ja: 幻覚？そこにないものが見えるってこと？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, exactly. That's the word. Is it too early for that?
script.ja: ええ、まさに。その言葉です。それって、もうそんな段階なんですかね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Maybe you just need more coffee.
script.ja: もしかしたら、もっとコーヒーが必要なだけかもね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Speaking of bad enough... My baby is almost one now. He's starting to really... insist on things. Like, if he wants a toy, he *really* wants that toy.
script.ja: 大変なことといえば…もうすぐうちの子も1歳なんです。本当に…色々と主張し始めてるんですよ。例えば、おもちゃが欲しいとき、本当にそのおもちゃを欲しがるんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Right. They start to figure out they're separate from you. So they want to assert themselves.
script.ja: そうね。自分たちがあなたとは別の存在だと理解し始めるのよ。だから、自己主張したがるの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Assert themselves? Like, show they're in charge?
script.ja: 自己主張？自分が主導権を握ってるって示す感じですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: More like, show they have their own will. They're realizing they can choose, or not choose. The article mentioned this: 'toddlers are driven to assert themselves, to communicate their likes and dislikes.'
script.ja: それよりも、自分には自分の意思があるって示す感じね。選ぶことができる、選ばないこともできるって気づき始めるの。記事にもあったでしょ。「幼児は自己主張し、好き嫌いを伝えようとする」って。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, so that's why they become... mini dictators? I'm joking, but sometimes it feels like that. He just stares at me and then throws his spoon.
script.ja: ああ、だから…ちっちゃな独裁者になるのか。冗談ですけど、時々そう感じますよ。僕をじっと見て、それからスプーンを投げたりするんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's a common way to communicate when they don't have words yet. It's not personal, it's just trying to get a reaction. And figuring out what happens.
script.ja: 言葉がないうちは、よくあるコミュニケーション方法よ。個人的なものではなく、ただ反応を見ようとしてるだけ。何が起こるか確かめているの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: So I guess I need to start setting 'age-appropriate limits' already, right? Like the article said. Even if he's not two yet.
script.ja: じゃあ、もう『年齢に応じた制限』を始めないといけないってことですね？記事にもあったように。まだ2歳じゃないけど。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's always a good time to start. Gentle, consistent limits work best. Mine did the same. It passes.
script.ja: 始めるのに早すぎるってことはないわ。優しく、一貫した制限が一番効果的よ。うちの子もそうだった。落ち着くわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It passes. That's what everyone says. I hope so. Because I'm getting pretty good at running on fumes.
script.ja: 落ち着く、ですか。みんなそう言いますね。そうだといいな。だって、もうガス欠寸前で動いてるようなものですよ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You'll be fine. Just keep that coffee supply underway.
script.ja: 大丈夫よ。そのコーヒーの供給を『アンダーウェイ』にしとけば。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Underway? Like, keep it flowing? Is that what that means?
script.ja: アンダーウェイ？流れ続けてるってことですか？そういう意味？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, 'underway' means something is in progress, or moving forward. Like a ship underway. Keep the coffee process going.
script.ja: ええ、『アンダーウェイ』は、何かが進行中であるとか、前に進んでいるという意味よ。航海中の船とかね。コーヒーの供給プロセスを止めないで、ってこと。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. Keep the coffee supply line... not broken. Got it. Well, thanks for the tips, Sarah. And the vocabulary.
script.ja: ああ、なるほど。コーヒーの供給ラインを…途絶えさせない。分かりました。サラさん、ヒントと語彙をありがとう。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: No problem, Alex. Another day, another adventure.
script.ja: どういたしまして、アレックス。また一日、また新たな冒険ね。

wikipedia.text: The first EF5 tornado in 12 years occurs in Enderlin, North Dakota.

parenting.title: Toddlers and Challenging Behavior: Why They Do It and How to Respond
parenting.summary: This article explores the meaning behind challenging behaviors and how parents and caregivers can set age-appropriate limits for their toddlers. The year between age 2 and age 3 is an exciting one. Toddlers are realizing that they are separate individuals from their parents and caregivers. This means that they are driven to assert themselves, to communicate their likes and dislikes, […]</
